title: Change in Islam Must Come from the Muslims Themselves
summary: It isn't often that a mainstream newspaper calls out Islam over a terrorist action—in this case one of the worst in history—the November 13, 2015 attacks in Paris—which ended in the murders of 129 people.
body: It isn't often that a mainstream newspaper calls out Islam over a terrorist action—in this case one of the worst in history—the November 13, 2015 attacks in Paris—which ended in the murders of 129 people. Instead of making the usual disclaimer that Islam as a whole should not be blamed, France's leading newspaper, Le Monde , had this to say in its lead editorial on November 17: "Will jihadism be conquered by [military, special forces, police and intelligence]? We must not lie to public opinion. The dismantlement alone of the Islamic State will not be enough because it stems from a pathology proper to Islam, and because islamism is a totalitarian ideology it will have to be defeated by the Muslims themselves." Until the mass of Muslims come to realize the full horror of the murders of innocent people that had been committed in their name, and they do something about it, there will be no rest. Statements and views expressed in this commentary are solely those of the author and do not imply endorsement by Harvard University, the Harvard Kennedy School, or the Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs. result.main_content_en: The article discusses the response to the November 2015 Paris attacks, highlighting a departure from typical media disclaimers. It notes that France's leading newspaper, Le Monde, argued in an editorial that military and security measures alone are insufficient to defeat jihadism. The editorial posits that the threat stems from a pathology within Islam itself and that Islamism, as a totalitarian ideology, must ultimately be confronted and defeated by Muslims. The core argument is that lasting change and the defeat of extremist ideology require internal recognition and action from the Muslim community, particularly in rejecting the violence committed in its name.
result.main_content_zh: 本文围绕2015年11月巴黎恐怖袭击事件后的舆论反应展开。文章指出，法国主流报纸《世界报》在其社论中打破了惯常的免责声明，并未简单宣称不应谴责整个伊斯兰教。相反，社论认为，仅靠军事、特种部队、警察和情报手段无法战胜圣战主义。其核心论点是，伊斯兰国的瓦解并不足够，因为问题根源在于伊斯兰内部的一种病态，而伊斯兰主义作为一种极权主义意识形态，必须由穆斯林自身来击败。文章强调，除非广大穆斯林充分认识到以他们名义杀害无辜者的恐怖行径，并采取行动，否则将永无宁日。
